message: Jake
Jennifer
I've missed you.
How have you been?
Since that happened to Cindy,
I don't know what to do.
I'm glad you came.
Ah,
you came.

It seems filming was canceled, and they were going to leave New York. The luggage is already all packed.
It looks like a room designed for long-term stays, consisting of a living room and bedroom. The lodging isn't cheap.
It's a perfect location overlooking 5th Avenue, but right now, I just don't feel like enjoying the view.
It seems she is strongly shocked by Cindy's death. Her face looks a bit pale, and there's a sense of anxiety in the air.
Maybe it's because I'm here, but she's wearing very light makeup and casual clothes. It makes me feel a sense of closeness.
Perhaps it's because she was quite close to Cindy, but today Jennifer seems a bit worn out.
I didn't even know Cindy was gone. So, I was surprised to hear it on the news.
It really was a tragic story.
You must have known something, right?
It's just been one unpleasant thing after another; it really gets you down.
That's true.
Sometimes I really hate being born in such a time.
It’s supposed to be a nice holiday, but it’s not turning into a fun vacation.
I haven't been in touch with Meryl. But she must have been somewhat shocked too; after all, they were co-stars.
Yeah, that's right.
To be honest, I'm not really on good terms with Meryl. So, I wouldn't reach out to her.
I wonder what Meryl is doing right now? I think she’s probably bored since the shoot was canceled.
If you'd like, I could be a listening ear.
I thought so, but she probably would shy away, for sure.
That's true.
If I were to show up, it would definitely seem strange.
As if you want me to go see her.
It's strange to say that knowing our relationship isn't good.
When they said Gloria was sick, it was because she was gone. I can't believe she was killed so long ago.
That's true.
But she wasn't killed at that time.
Oh, really? Then what about...?
Cindy slipped away from Gloria's place to go to her boyfriend's. Recently, she's gone missing.
Why? Why did she escape from Gloria's?
What happened between the two?
Maybe you know the reason better than anyone?
Huh, me!? Why?
You were close with Cindy, right? So, I thought maybe you received some advice from her.
I.
It's true that I was close with Cindy, but we haven't really talked much lately.
I see.
Now that I think about it, during the party, she seemed like she wanted to say something.
I arrived late back then, so we didn't have a chance to talk.
Ah, to think I couldn't say anything and it turned out like this.
It's true that regret comes too late.
The news keeps reporting as if Gai is the culprit.
Yeah.
With that, everyone who saw it would think Gai is the criminal.
It's okay.
Gai surely isn't the culprit. So, don't get discouraged.
It must be tough.
But, really, don't let it get you down.
It's alright. I don't think Gai's the criminal.
You trust your friend.
Since we've known each other for a long time, I believe he's not the kind of guy who could do something so drastic.
Right.
That's true. It's nice to have that kind of relationship.
Having friends you can truly trust is wonderful.
Cindy's case might have its possibilities, right?
We still don’t know.
But.
It feels somehow scary.
You don't think I might be the culprit, do you?
Why?
Because there are many people like that. Even now, somewhere, someone might be saying I'm the culprit.
It's like that.
No, that's not true. That's paranoia.
Because
It’s been that way till now. No one cares about me.
But you believe in me, right?
I didn't take any other work for this movie.
So, I'm free for a while.
I see.
It should have been a pleasant, laid-back life.
It's awful that it has come to this.
What am I supposed to do?
You should take it easy. You're in demand right now, so work will keep coming, right?
Is that so?
Being in demand can be fleeting. It’s a transient thing.
I hope work goes well from now on.
She must have been shocked to hear the news about Cindy. She seems a bit worn out.
If it was the one who killed Cindy,
wouldn't they be surprised like this?
Oh, she's already leaving New York. I have only a few days left to see her.
Not having a female friend to help her when it matters is something Meryl should be sad about.
She seemed to be quite disturbed.
I hope she doesn’t end up thinking of killing Guy out of desperation.
Should I go check on her? I can't predict what Meryl might do right now.
I wonder why the culprit chose Cindy. Was it because she was beautiful? Was he her fan? Or was there another reason?
It's so sad that a talented young girl like Cindy has lost her life.
Let's follow Cindy's footprints until she gets killed. We might get testimony that connects to the culprit.
No matter what the reason is, I can't imagine Guy killing someone. I wonder why Guy isn't making excuses.
Is there something behind Guy not trying to prove his innocence? I'll have to meet him to find out.
Whether he committed murder or is innocent, it's undeniable that Guy is still hurting Kristy. I can't forgive that.
He easily dodges investigations by the police and FBI; he must be really smart.
Why does he repeat multiple offenses in New York? Why is he so attached to this city that he'd take such risks?
It's a bit strange that, after so many incidents, the true identity of the culprit isn't surfacing.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
